event_description_en: Presumably, so much water was added from the other side that the safety valve on the system opened and flooded the basement.

event_description_en: The DHW controller was set to night mode. Reset to automatic.
Externally controlled, supply temperature limited to 55°C.
Several warning messages keep appearing: ‘Alarm leak sensor’. Sensor is probably defective.
The heaters are not getting warm enough. Supply temperature is 44°C at 9°C outdoor temperature. Heating curve increased.
Flow temperature too low and increased to 55°C.
Optimization of controller parameters led to insufficient supply for heating. Control system reset. Air in circulation for DHW, vented.
Only 40 °C heating supply. Heating curve increased. Controller now reaches 62 degrees supply.

Insufficient heat in the building system; heating circuit 1 shows no cooling and control limits primary side return temperature, probably short circuit in the connected object. Heating circuit 2 is not receiving sufficient volume flow. The pump output has therefore been increased.
